name: Gradient Descent
tagline: Iterative optimization using gradients to reduce an objective
problem:
Many objectives are too complex to solve in one step. You need an iterative method that improves a solution gradually using local information.

Gradient descent provides a generic workhorse: use the gradient as a direction of improvement and take steps with a learning rate. The hard part is step size, noise, and conditioning.
overview:
Basic gradient descent updates parameters by subtracting a scaled gradient. Variants include momentum and adaptive methods. Stochastic versions use minibatches to scale to large datasets.

In engineering, the same idea appears as iterative refinement. In AI, it is the engine that trains neural networks when combined with backpropagation.

principles.name: Learning rate
principles.explanation: Too large diverges; too small stalls. Schedules and adaptive steps manage tradeoffs.

principles.name: Stochastic approximation
principles.explanation: Noisy gradients can still converge with correct averaging and step sizes.

principles.name: Conditioning
principles.explanation: Ill-conditioned problems require preconditioning or careful normalization.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

rawMaterials.name: Semiconductor devices
rawMaterials.purpose: Transistors and ICs that perform switching, storage, and signal conditioning
earthLocations: Global semiconductor supply chains; packaged IC distributors
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Early colonies import packaged devices; long-term local manufacture requires clean chemistry and crystal growth capabilities.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: ESD-safe handling; correct packaging, heat sinking, and derating for temperature and radiation.

rawMaterials.name: Documentation and standards
rawMaterials.purpose: Make designs reproducible and debuggable across teams and time
earthLocations: Libraries, universities, standards bodies, archived web
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Mirror core references offline; maintain printed and digital copies with checksums and redundancy.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Versioning, change control, and clear measurement units and assumptions.

rawMaterials.name: Paper and writing media
rawMaterials.purpose: Record calculations, assumptions, and experimental outcomes
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Digital notes plus printed backups; radiation-safe storage for critical references.

buildSteps.title: Define objective
buildSteps.description: Write the loss you want to minimize and the parameters it depends on.

buildSteps.title: Compute gradients
buildSteps.description: Derive or approximate gradients; verify on small examples.

buildSteps.title: Choose step size and normalization
buildSteps.description: Start conservative; normalize inputs; use schedules if needed.

buildSteps.title: Monitor convergence
buildSteps.description: Track objective, validation metrics, and instability indicators.
buildSteps.warningNote: Training loss decreasing does not guarantee generalization.

history.event: Gradient-based methods become standard in numerical optimization.
Stochastic gradient descent becomes central to large-scale learning.
Adaptive methods and large-scale training popularize variants.

impact:
Gradient descent makes optimization practical at scale and enables modern ML training. It also creates new failure modes: instability, overfitting, and brittle dependence on hyperparameters.

In The Codex, it is a core algorithm node that unlocks deep learning when paired with backpropagation.

sources.title: Goodfellow et al. — Deep Learning (optimization chapter)
